cards.grammarNote: كَثِير (kathīr) = a lot/much. When followed by a specific noun, use مِن (min, 'of'): كَثِير مِن الْمَاء. كَثِيرًا is the adverbial form: شَكَرًا كَثِيرًا (thank you very much).
قَلِيل (qalīl) = a little/few. Opposite of كَثِير. Also used adverbially: تَكَلَّمْ قَلِيلًا (speak a little). For uncountable nouns use مِن + the noun with the definite article.
بَعْض (baʿḍ) = some/part of. It is followed directly by a definite noun (with الـ) in the genitive: بَعْض الطَّعَام (some food). Unlike كَثِير and قَلِيل, it does not use مِن.

كِلَا (kilā) = both (for dual masculine), كِلْتَا (kiltā) = both (for dual feminine). Always followed by a dual noun in the genitive. كِلَا الطَّبَقَيْنِ = both dishes (lit. 'both of the two dishes').
نِصْف (niṣf) = half. Directly precedes the noun without مِن: نِصْف كِيلُو (half a kilo), نِصْف سَاعَة (half an hour). The following noun is in the genitive case.
كَافٍ (kāfin) = sufficient/enough, an adjective that follows the noun and agrees in gender. Alternatively: يَكْفِي الطَّعَام (the food is sufficient) using the verb كَفَى.
When نِصْف is followed by a definite noun, the noun takes the definite article الـ: نِصْف الْوَجْبَة (half the meal). When indefinite: نِصْف وَجْبَة (half a meal).
